raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/2024 I ordered from a local restaurant known as the Boiling Crab. I attempted to submit a dispute with Citi because the food was inedible and I could not reach the restaurant by phone nor email for a refund. I was unable to dispute the transaction using my Citi mobile app due to it being a " food '' charge according to Citi Bank. It took over an hour and several different transfers and being given the run around to finally speak to a representative. The dispute was opened and then was closed about a month later. Citi closed the dispute and sent me a " letter '' explaining it was because they needed additional info from me, I never got that letter. I reopened the dispute AGAIN and faced the same issues, basically identical. Citi then proceeds to send me another letter stating they need more information from me but I never received said letter to my home. They then closed the dispute AGAIN because of lack of information. I spoke to well over 5 representatives and explained my problem very clearly and demonstrated that I was unable to contact the restaurant and they still refuse to refund me for my purchase. I want the charge to be reserved and to receive a full refund, I was unable to eat the food that was provided and I should not have to write a letter explaining the same exact information that I already shared with them SEVERAL TIMES on MULTIPLE OCCASIONS to receive a refund.
